schema.targetId: stable kebab-case id derived from the skill display name
schema.targetName: exact skill display name used by progress keys
schema.levels: object with keys 1, 2, 3; each level contains exactly three required main quests

intro.title: Meet access control
intro.description: Study an introductory reference for access control, then summarize what it is, when it is used, and one small practical action a beginner can try.

1.title: Identify access control Basics
1.description: Read the beginner material for access control, then explain the core terms a new teammate must understand before using it.

1.title: Map a Simple access control Use Case
1.description: Describe a small real-world situation where access control helps, including the input, action, and expected result.

1.title: Avoid a Common access control Mistake
1.description: Find one beginner mistake related to access control, then summarize how to notice it and how to avoid it.

2.title: Apply access control in Context
2.description: Use the reference to design a practical workflow for access control, then explain why each step is needed.

2.title: Compare access control Tradeoffs
2.description: Write a comparison between a simple approach and a more structured approach for access control, including when each is appropriate.

2.title: Troubleshoot access control
2.description: Create a short troubleshooting checklist for access control, focusing on symptoms, likely causes, and safe next steps.

3.title: Design a Reusable access control Practice
3.description: Turn access control into a repeatable practice that another teammate could follow, including review points and quality checks.

3.title: Review access control Like a Senior
3.description: Audit a access control decision for correctness, maintainability, and risk, then summarize what you would approve or change.

3.title: Prove access control Mastery
3.description: Create a small proof-of-understanding artifact for access control, such as notes, a checklist, a mini demo, or a before-and-after explanation. Show how permissions protect actions and data, not only login screens.

intro.title: Meet Version Control
intro.description: Learn what version control is, why IT teams use it, and write a short summary of how it protects project work from accidental loss.

1.title: Create a Local Repository
1.description: Study how a repository starts, then explain the difference between a normal folder and a folder tracked by version control.

links.label: Git Book - Getting a Git Repository

1.title: Record a Small Change
1.description: Learn the add-and-commit flow, then summarize what staging means and why a commit should describe one coherent change.

1.title: Read Project History
1.description: Review how history is inspected, then write what information a teammate can learn from commit history before changing a file.

2.title: Explain Branch Purpose
2.description: Study branches, then summarize how a branch lets someone work on a feature without disturbing the main line of work.

links.label: Git Book - Branches in a Nutshell

2.title: Practice Branch and Merge Thinking
2.description: Read the basic branch-and-merge flow, then describe a realistic case where merging is safer than editing directly on the main branch.

links.label: Git Book - Basic Branching and Merging

2.title: Recover from a Local Mistake
2.description: Study reset behavior at a conceptual level, then summarize how someone should think before undoing local work.

3.title: Review History Rewriting Risk
3.description: Study history rewriting, then explain when rewriting history is useful and when it can harm collaborators.

3.title: Analyze a Hard Merge
3.description: Read about advanced merging, then write a checklist for investigating a merge conflict before choosing a resolution.

3.title: Prepare a Contribution Flow
3.description: Study distributed contribution workflows, then summarize how a clean branch, reviewable commits, and a pull request support teamwork.

links.label: Git Book - Contributing to a Project
